fingerprint: claim|claim-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-clinical-pattern|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|carpal tunnel syndrome evaluation combines the symptom pattern focused median nerve examination

object.claimText: Carpal tunnel syndrome evaluation combines the symptom pattern, focused median nerve examination, and selective diagnostic testing rather than relying on routine imaging.

metadata.sourceNote: CTS vertical preparation; source before manufacture

fingerprint: claim|claim-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-anatomy|median-nerve|carpal tunnel syndrome localizes to the median nerve within the carpal tunnel release planning a

object.claimText: Carpal tunnel syndrome localizes to the median nerve within the carpal tunnel; release planning also requires review of median nerve branch anatomy.

metadata.sourceNote: CTS vertical preparation; attending anatomy review required

fingerprint: claim|claim-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-exam|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|document sensory distribution thenar motor findings and provocative maneuvers while considering

object.claimText: Document sensory distribution, thenar motor findings, and provocative maneuvers while considering proximal median neuropathy, cervical radiculopathy, and other mimics.

metadata.sourceNote: CTS vertical preparation; source before manufacture

fingerprint: claim|claim-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-testing|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|electrodiagnostic testing is a selective diagnostic and severity tool mri and repeat radiographs

object.claimText: Electrodiagnostic testing is a selective diagnostic and severity tool; MRI and repeat radiographs are not routine CTS follow-up tests.

metadata.sourceNote: CTS vertical preparation; EDX specialist review required

fingerprint: claim|claim-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-treatment-options|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|cts treatment planning distinguishes neutral position night splinting and corticosteroid injecti

object.claimText: CTS treatment planning distinguishes neutral-position night splinting and corticosteroid injection from named open and endoscopic release procedures.

metadata.sourceNote: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

fingerprint: claim|claim-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-complications|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|persistent symptoms recurrent symptoms incomplete release pillar pain and iatrogenic nerve injur

object.claimText: Persistent symptoms, recurrent symptoms, incomplete release, pillar pain, and iatrogenic nerve injury are distinct concepts and should not be collapsed into a generic complication.

metadata.sourceNote: CTS vertical preparation; attending review required

fingerprint: claim|claim-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-postoperative|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|post release activity therapy and return to work guidance should be individualized routine fract

object.claimText: Post-release activity, therapy, and return-to-work guidance should be individualized; routine fracture-style immobilization and repeat radiographs do not apply.

metadata.sourceNote: CTS vertical preparation; attending and rehabilitation review required

fingerprint: claim|claim-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-oite-trap|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|oite content should distinguish palmar cutaneous and recurrent motor branch anatomy selective te

object.claimText: OITE content should distinguish palmar cutaneous and recurrent motor branch anatomy, selective test use, and persistent symptoms from recurrent disease and incomplete release.

metadata.sourceNote: CTS vertical preparation; educator and domain review required

fingerprint: decision_point|dp-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-testing|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|atypical presentation uncertain localization or severity competi

object.trigger: Atypical presentation, uncertain localization or severity, competing diagnosis, or a result expected to change management
object.action: Consider targeted electrodiagnostic or other supported testing; do not default to routine radiographs or MRI

fingerprint: decision_point|dp-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-nonoperative|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|presentation appropriate for an initial nonoperative pathway wit

object.trigger: Presentation appropriate for an initial nonoperative pathway without an attending-reviewed escalation indication
object.action: Discuss education, neutral-position night splinting, and/or corticosteroid injection with source-specific expectations and follow-up

fingerprint: decision_point|dp-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-operative|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|persistent function limiting symptoms after appropriate care sev

object.trigger: Persistent function-limiting symptoms after appropriate care, severe disease, or objective motor or denervation findings
object.action: Discuss a named carpal tunnel release option after confirming diagnosis, severity, patient factors, and goals

fingerprint: decision_point|dp-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-approach-choice|carpal-tunnel-syndrome|a reviewed surgical indication is present and open versus endosc

object.trigger: A reviewed surgical indication is present and open versus endoscopic technique is being selected
object.action: Use shared decision-making informed by anatomy, prior surgery, risks, resources, patient preference, and surgeon expertise

fingerprint: decision_point|dp-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-endoscopic-bailout|endoscopic-carpal-tunnel-release|inadequate visualization anomalous anatomy bleeding or another s

object.trigger: Inadequate visualization, anomalous anatomy, bleeding, or another safety concern during endoscopic release
object.action: Stop or convert according to an attending-approved bailout protocol

fingerprint: decision_point|dp-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-persistent-symptoms|persistent-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-after-release|symptoms do not adequately improve after carpal tunnel release

object.trigger: Symptoms do not adequately improve after carpal tunnel release
object.action: Reassess diagnosis, localization, severity, and release completeness before considering revision

fingerprint: decision_point|dp-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-recurrent-symptoms|recurrent-carpal-tunnel-syndrome|cts symptoms return after a period of improvement following rele

object.trigger: CTS symptoms return after a period of improvement following release
object.action: Evaluate recurrent compression and alternative causes before considering revision

fingerprint: decision_point|dp-carpal-tunnel-syndrome-postoperative-escalation|carpal-tunnel-release-postoperative-protocol|progressive neurologic deficit vascular concern wound concern or

object.trigger: Progressive neurologic deficit, vascular concern, wound concern, or severe unexpected postoperative pain
object.action: Obtain prompt surgical assessment under the treating team's protocol
